message.content:
Adversaries may exploit information repositories to mine valuable data, leveraging these tools to facilitate collaboration or information sharing. Common repositories include platforms like SharePoint, Confluence, code repositories, IaaS databases, and SQL Servers. Such repositories can store a wide variety of data, including sensitive corporate information, user accounts, and schemas, which adversaries can use to further their objectives or gain direct access to target information. Notable groups like APT28, APT29, FIN6, Fox Kitten, LAPSUS$, P.A.S. Webshell, and Turla have been known to exploit these repositories, accessing internal knowledge bases, security environments, and collaboration channels to extract valuable data.

Mitigation strategies include implementing strict access controls, monitoring and logging repository access, employing data encryption, and regularly auditing repository contents. Additionally, organizations should disable unnecessary external sharing features and educate employees on the risks of information repositories to reduce the likelihood of exploitation.
